propositions: When main advances, signed plan-review attestations read stale and each re-review of a byte-identical plan runs the full un-floored criteria set, exposing ~32% same-material verdict instability.

The progressive drift-refresh feature runs a cheap scoped E4+G1G2 grounding probe against drifted code and refreshes the attestation when the plan still holds, but shipped default OFF pending an untaken token/latency measurement.

The default should flip progressive_drift_refresh from False to True so drift-only-stale re-reviews take the scoped probe path instead of a full re-review, with explicit False restoring the full-re-review back-out.
A drift-only-stale re-review under default config must run only the E4/G1G2 probe scope and refresh a still-grounded attestation without re-running the full criteria set.
The probe fail-safes to a full re-review on probe error, registry-version skew, or no reusable prior verdict, preserving correctness.
Stale default-off framing about drift-refresh must be swept from src, tests, and docs, and the structural ~83% agent-criterion reduction recorded as the authorization basis.
